metadata.summary: pytest xdist plugin for distributed testing, most importantly across multiple CPUs

documentation:
# pytest-xdist

```{image} http://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pytest-xdist.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-xdist
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/pytest-xdist.svg
:target: https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/pytest-xdist
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pytest-xdist.svg
:alt: Python versions
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-xdist
```

```{image} https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-xdist/workflows/test/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-xdist/actions
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:target: https://github.com/ambv/black
```

The [pytest-xdist] plugin extends pytest with new test execution modes, the most used being distributing
tests across multiple CPUs to speed up test execution:

```
pytest -n auto
```

With this call, pytest will spawn a number of workers processes equal to the number of available CPUs, and distribute
the tests randomly across them.

## Documentation

Documentation is available at [Read The Docs](https://pytest-xdist.readthedocs.io).

modules.name: xdist._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: xdist._path.visit_path
documentation:
Implements the interface of `py.path.local.visit()` for Path objects,
to simplify porting the code over from `py.path.local`.

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.loadgroup.LoadGroupScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadgroup.LoadGroupScheduling._split_scope
documentation:
Determine the scope (grouping) of a nodeid.

There are usually 3 cases for a nodeid:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py::test_beta0
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1::test_delta0
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py::epsilon.epsilon
```

1. Function in a test module.
2. Method of a class in a test module.
3. Doctest in a function in a package.

With loadgroup, two cases are added:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py::test_beta0
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1::test_delta0
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py::epsilon.epsilon
example/loadsuite/test/test_gamma.py::test_beta0@gname
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Gamma1::test_gamma0@gname
```

This function will group tests with the scope determined by splitting the first `@`
from the right. That is, test will be grouped in a single work unit when they have
same group name. In the above example, scopes will be:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py::test_beta0
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1::test_delta0
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py::epsilon.epsilon
gname
gname
```

Implement load scheduling across nodes, but grouping test by xdist_group mark.

This class behaves very much like LoadScopeScheduling, but it groups tests by xdist_group mark
instead of the module or class to which they belong to.

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.each.EachScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.each.EachScheduling.nodes
documentation: A list of all nodes in the scheduler.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.each.EachScheduling.has_pending
documentation:
Return True if there are pending test items.

This indicates that collection has finished and nodes are
still processing test items, so this can be thought of as
"the scheduler is active".

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.each.EachScheduling.add_node_collection
documentation:
Add the collected test items from a node.

Collection is complete once all nodes have submitted their
collection.  In this case its pending list is set to an empty
list.  When the collection is already completed this
submission is from a node which was restarted to replace a
dead node.  In this case we already assign the pending items
here.  In either case `.schedule()` will instruct the
node to start running the required tests.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.each.EachScheduling.schedule
documentation:
Schedule the test items on the nodes.

If the node's pending list is empty it is a new node which
needs to run all the tests.  If the pending list is already
populated (by `.add_node_collection()`) then it replaces a
dead node and we only need to run those tests.

Implement scheduling of test items on all nodes.

If a node gets added after the test run is started then it is
assumed to replace a node which got removed before it finished
its collection.  In this case it will only be used if a node
with the same spec got removed earlier.

Any nodes added after the run is started will only get items
assigned if a node with a matching spec was removed before it
finished all its pending items.  The new node will then be
assigned the remaining items from the removed node.

modules.name: xdist.remote
documentation:
This module is executed in remote subprocesses and helps to
control a remote testing session and relay back information.
It assumes that 'py' is importable and does not have dependencies
on the rest of the xdist code.  This means that the xdist-plugin
needs not to be installed in remote environments.

classes.name: xdist.remote.Producer
documentation:
Simplified implementation of the same interface as py.log, for backward compatibility
since we dropped the dependency on pylib.
Note: this is defined here because this module can't depend on xdist, so we need
to have the other way around.

methods.name: xdist.remote.WorkerInteractor._get_next_item_index
documentation:
Gets the next item from test queue. Handles the case when the queue
is replaced concurrently in another thread.

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.loadfile.LoadFileScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadfile.LoadFileScheduling._split_scope
documentation:
Determine the scope (grouping) of a nodeid.

There are usually 3 cases for a nodeid:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py::test_beta0
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1::test_delta0
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py::epsilon.epsilon
```

1. Function in a test module.
2. Method of a class in a test module.
3. Doctest in a function in a package.

This function will group tests with the scope determined by splitting
the first `::` from the left. That is, test will be grouped in a
single work unit when they reside in the same file.

In the above example, scopes will be:

```
.. code-block:: text
```

> example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py
> example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py
> example/loadsuite/epsilon/\_\_init\_\_.py

Implement load scheduling across nodes, but grouping test test file.

This distributes the tests collected across all nodes so each test is run
just once.  All nodes collect and submit the list of tests and when all
collections are received it is verified they are identical collections.
Then the collection gets divided up in work units, grouped by test file,
and those work units get submitted to nodes.  Whenever a node finishes an
item, it calls `.mark_test_complete()` which will trigger the scheduler
to assign more work units if the number of pending tests for the node falls
below a low-watermark.

When created, `numnodes` defines how many nodes are expected to submit a
collection. This is used to know when all nodes have finished collection.

This class behaves very much like LoadScopeScheduling, but with a file-level scope.

the other side and if the channel closes.

Note that channel callbacks run in the receiver
thread of execnet gateways - we need to

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.nodes
documentation: A list of all active nodes in the scheduler.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.collection_is_completed
documentation:
Boolean indication initial test collection is complete.

This is a boolean indicating all initial participating nodes have
finished collection.  The required number of initial nodes is defined
by `.numnodes`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.tests_finished
documentation: Return True if all tests have been executed by the nodes.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.has_pending
documentation:
Return True if there are pending test items.

This indicates that collection has finished and nodes are still
processing test items, so this can be thought of as
"the scheduler is active".

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.add_node
documentation:
Add a new node to the scheduler.

From now on the node will be assigned work units to be executed.

Called by the `DSession.worker_workerready` hook when it successfully
bootstraps a new node.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.remove_node
documentation:
Remove a node from the scheduler.

This should be called either when the node crashed or at shutdown time.
In the former case any pending items assigned to the node will be
re-scheduled.

Called by the hooks:

- `DSession.worker_workerfinished`.
- `DSession.worker_errordown`.

Return the item being executed while the node crashed or None if the
node has no more pending items.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.add_node_collection
documentation:
Add the collected test items from a node.

The collection is stored in the `.registered_collections` dictionary.

Called by the hook:

- `DSession.worker_collectionfinish`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.mark_test_complete
documentation:
Mark test item as completed by node.

Called by the hook:

- `DSession.worker_testreport`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling._assign_work_unit
documentation: Assign a work unit to a node.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling._split_scope
documentation:
Determine the scope (grouping) of a nodeid.

There are usually 3 cases for a nodeid:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py::test_beta0
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1::test_delta0
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py::epsilon.epsilon
```

1. Function in a test module.
2. Method of a class in a test module.
3. Doctest in a function in a package.

This function will group tests with the scope determined by splitting
the first `::` from the right. That is, classes will be grouped in a
single work unit, and functions from a test module will be grouped by
their module. In the above example, scopes will be:

```
example/loadsuite/test/test_beta.py
example/loadsuite/test/test_delta.py::Delta1
example/loadsuite/epsilon/__init__.py
```

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling._pending_of
documentation: Return the number of pending tests in a workload.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling._reschedule
documentation:
Maybe schedule new items on the node.

If there are any globally pending work units left then this will check
if the given node should be given any more tests.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling.schedule
documentation:
Initiate distribution of the test collection.

Initiate scheduling of the items across the nodes.  If this gets called
again later it behaves the same as calling `._reschedule()` on all
nodes so that newly added nodes will start to be used.

If `.collection_is_completed` is True, this is called by the hook:

- `DSession.worker_collectionfinish`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.loadscope.LoadScopeScheduling._check_nodes_have_same_collection
documentation:
Return True if all nodes have collected the same items.

If collections differ, this method returns False while logging
the collection differences and posting collection errors to
pytest_collectreport hook.

Implement load scheduling across nodes, but grouping test by scope.

This distributes the tests collected across all nodes so each test is run
just once.  All nodes collect and submit the list of tests and when all
collections are received it is verified they are identical collections.
Then the collection gets divided up in work units, grouped by test scope,
and those work units get submitted to nodes.  Whenever a node finishes an
item, it calls `.mark_test_complete()` which will trigger the scheduler
to assign more work units if the number of pending tests for the node falls
below a low-watermark.

When created, `numnodes` defines how many nodes are expected to submit a
collection. This is used to know when all nodes have finished collection.

Attributes:

```
:numnodes: The expected number of nodes taking part.  The actual number of
```

> nodes will vary during the scheduler's lifetime as nodes are added by
> the DSession as they are brought up and removed either because of a dead
> node or normal shutdown.  This number is primarily used to know when the
> initial collection is completed.

```
collection

The final list of tests collected by all nodes once it is
validated to be identical between all the nodes.  It is initialised to
None until .schedule() is called.

workqueue

Ordered dictionary that maps all available scopes with their
associated tests (nodeid). Nodeids are in turn associated with their
completion status. One entry of the workqueue is called a work unit.
In turn, a collection of work unit is called a workload.

workqueue = {
    '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py': {
        '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case1': False,
        '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case2': False,
        (...)
    },
    (...)
}

assigned_work

Ordered dictionary that maps worker nodes with their
assigned work units.

assigned_work = {
    '<worker node A>': {
        '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py': {
            '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case1': False,
            '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case2': False,
            (...)
        },
        (...)
    },
    (...)
}

registered_collections

Ordered dictionary that maps worker nodes with
their collection of tests gathered during test discovery.

registered_collections = {
    '<worker node A>': [
        '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case1',
        '<full>/<path>/<to>/test_module.py::test_case2',
    ],
    (...)
}

log

A py.log.Producer instance.

config

Config object, used for handling hooks.
```

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.nodes
documentation: A list of all nodes in the scheduler.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.collection_is_completed
documentation:
Boolean indication initial test collection is complete.

This is a boolean indicating all initial participating nodes
have finished collection.  The required number of initial
nodes is defined by `.numnodes`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.tests_finished
documentation: Return True if all tests have been executed by the nodes.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.has_pending
documentation:
Return True if there are pending test items.

This indicates that collection has finished and nodes are
still processing test items, so this can be thought of as
"the scheduler is active".

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.add_node
documentation:
Add a new node to the scheduler.

From now on the node will be allocated chunks of tests to
execute.

Called by the `DSession.worker_workerready` hook when it
successfully bootstraps a new node.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.add_node_collection
documentation:
Add the collected test items from a node.

The collection is stored in the `.node2collection` map.
Called by the `DSession.worker_collectionfinish` hook.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.mark_test_complete
documentation:
Mark test item as completed by node.

This is called by the `DSession.worker_testreport` hook.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.remove_pending_tests_from_node
documentation:
Node returned some test indices back in response to 'steal' command.

This is called by `DSession.worker_unscheduled`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.check_schedule
documentation: Reschedule tests/perform load balancing.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.remove_node
documentation:
Remove a node from the scheduler.

This should be called either when the node crashed or at
shutdown time.  In the former case any pending items assigned
to the node will be re-scheduled.  Called by the
`DSession.worker_workerfinished` and
`DSession.worker_errordown` hooks.

Return the item which was being executing while the node
crashed or None if the node has no more pending items.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling.schedule
documentation:
Initiate distribution of the test collection.

Initiate scheduling of the items across the nodes.  If this
gets called again later it behaves the same as calling
`.check_schedule()` on all nodes so that newly added nodes
will start to be used.

This is called by the `DSession.worker_collectionfinish` hook
if `.collection_is_completed` is True.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.worksteal.WorkStealingScheduling._check_nodes_have_same_collection
documentation:
Return True if all nodes have collected the same items.

If collections differ, this method returns False while logging
the collection differences and posting collection errors to
pytest_collectreport hook.

Implement work-stealing scheduling.

Initially, tests are distributed evenly among all nodes.

When some node completes most of its assigned tests (when only one pending
test remains), an attempt is made to reassign ("steal") some tests from
other nodes to this node.

Attributes:

```
:numnodes: The expected number of nodes taking part.  The actual
```

> number of nodes will vary during the scheduler's lifetime as
> nodes are added by the DSession as they are brought up and
> removed either because of a dead node or normal shutdown.  This
> number is primarily used to know when the initial collection is
> completed.

```
node2collection

Map of nodes and their test collection.  All
collections should always be identical.

node2pending

Map of nodes and the indices of their pending
tests.  The indices are an index into .pending (which is
identical to their own collection stored in
.node2collection).

collection

The one collection once it is validated to be
identical between all the nodes.  It is initialised to None
until .schedule() is called.

pending

List of indices of globally pending tests.  These are
tests which have not yet been allocated to a chunk for a node
to process.

log

A py.log.Producer instance.

config

Config object, used for handling hooks.

steal_requested_from_node

The node to which the current "steal" request
was sent. None if there is no request in progress. Only one request
can be in progress at any time, the scheduler doesn't send multiple
simultaneous requests.
```

modules.name: xdist.newhooks
documentation:
xdist hooks.

Additionally, pytest-xdist will also decorate a few other hooks
with the worker instance that executed the hook originally:

`pytest_runtest_logreport`: `rep` parameter has a `node` attribute.

You can use this hooks just as you would use normal pytest hooks, but some care
must be taken in plugins in case `xdist` is not installed. Please see:

> <http://pytest.org/en/latest/writing_plugins.html#optionally-using-hooks-from-3rd-party-plugins>

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_setupnodes
documentation: Called before any remote node is set up.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_newgateway
documentation: Called on new raw gateway creation.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_rsyncstart
documentation: Called before rsyncing a directory to remote gateways takes place.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_rsyncfinish
documentation: Called after rsyncing a directory to remote gateways takes place.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_getremotemodule
documentation: Called when creating remote node.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_configure_node
documentation: Configure node information before it gets instantiated.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_testnodeready
documentation: Test Node is ready to operate.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_testnodedown
documentation: Test Node is down.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_node_collection_finished
documentation: Called by the controller node when a worker node finishes collecting.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_make_scheduler
documentation: Return a node scheduler implementation.

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_xdist_auto_num_workers
documentation:
Return the number of workers to spawn when `--numprocesses=auto` is given in the
command-line.

:::{versionadded} 2.1
:::

functions.name: xdist.newhooks.pytest_handlecrashitem
documentation:
Handle a crashitem, modifying the report if necessary.

The scheduler is provided as a parameter to reschedule the test if desired with
`sched.mark_test_pending`.

def pytest_handlecrashitem(crashitem, report, sched):
: if should_rerun(crashitem):

  : sched.mark_test_pending(crashitem)
    report.outcome = "rerun"

:::{versionadded} 2.2.1
:::

modules.name: xdist.looponfail
documentation:
Implement -f aka looponfailing for pytest.

NOTE that we try to avoid loading and depending on application modules
within the controlling process (the one that starts repeatedly test
processes) otherwise changes to source code can crash
the controlling process which should best never happen.

functions.name: xdist.plugin.pytest_addhooks
documentation: distributed testing hooks

functions.name: xdist.plugin.pytest_configure
documentation: distributed testing initialization

functions.name: xdist.plugin._is_distribution_mode
documentation: Whether distribution mode is on.

fixtures and API to easily know the role of current node

functions.name: xdist.plugin.is_xdist_controller
documentation:
Return `True` if this is the xdist controller, `False` otherwise.

Note: this method also returns `False` when distribution has not been
activated at all.

```
param request_or_session

the   or  object
```

functions.name: xdist.plugin.get_xdist_worker_id
documentation:
Return the id of the current worker ('gw0', 'gw1', etc) or 'master'
if running on the controller node.

If not distributing tests (for example passing `-n0` or not passing `-n` at all)
also return 'master'.

```
param request_or_session

the   or  object
```

functions.name: xdist.plugin.worker_id
documentation:
Return the id of the current worker ('gw0', 'gw1', etc) or 'master'
if running on the master node.

functions.name: xdist.plugin.testrun_uid
documentation: Return the unique id of the current test.

classes.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.nodes
documentation: A list of all nodes in the scheduler.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.collection_is_completed
documentation:
Boolean indication initial test collection is complete.

This is a boolean indicating all initial participating nodes
have finished collection.  The required number of initial
nodes is defined by `.numnodes`.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.tests_finished
documentation: Return True if all tests have been executed by the nodes.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.has_pending
documentation:
Return True if there are pending test items.

This indicates that collection has finished and nodes are
still processing test items, so this can be thought of as
"the scheduler is active".

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.add_node
documentation:
Add a new node to the scheduler.

From now on the node will be allocated chunks of tests to
execute.

Called by the `DSession.worker_workerready` hook when it
successfully bootstraps a new node.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.add_node_collection
documentation:
Add the collected test items from a node.

The collection is stored in the `.node2collection` map.
Called by the `DSession.worker_collectionfinish` hook.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.mark_test_complete
documentation:
Mark test item as completed by node.

The duration it took to execute the item is used as a hint to
the scheduler.

This is called by the `DSession.worker_testreport` hook.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.check_schedule
documentation:
Maybe schedule new items on the node.

If there are any globally pending nodes left then this will
check if the given node should be given any more tests.  The
`duration` of the last test is optionally used as a
heuristic to influence how many tests the node is assigned.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.remove_node
documentation:
Remove a node from the scheduler.

This should be called either when the node crashed or at
shutdown time.  In the former case any pending items assigned
to the node will be re-scheduled.  Called by the
`DSession.worker_workerfinished` and
`DSession.worker_errordown` hooks.

Return the item which was being executing while the node
crashed or None if the node has no more pending items.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling.schedule
documentation:
Initiate distribution of the test collection.

Initiate scheduling of the items across the nodes.  If this
gets called again later it behaves the same as calling
`.check_schedule()` on all nodes so that newly added nodes
will start to be used.

This is called by the `DSession.worker_collectionfinish` hook
if `.collection_is_completed` is True.

methods.name: xdist.scheduler.load.LoadScheduling._check_nodes_have_same_collection
documentation:
Return True if all nodes have collected the same items.

If collections differ, this method returns False while logging
the collection differences and posting collection errors to
pytest_collectreport hook.

Implement load scheduling across nodes.

This distributes the tests collected across all nodes so each test
is run just once.  All nodes collect and submit the test suite and
when all collections are received it is verified they are
identical collections.  Then the collection gets divided up in
chunks and chunks get submitted to nodes.  Whenever a node finishes
an item, it calls `.mark_test_complete()` which will trigger the
scheduler to assign more tests if the number of pending tests for
the node falls below a low-watermark.

When created, `numnodes` defines how many nodes are expected to
submit a collection. This is used to know when all nodes have
finished collection or how large the chunks need to be created.

Attributes:

```
:numnodes: The expected number of nodes taking part.  The actual
```

> number of nodes will vary during the scheduler's lifetime as
> nodes are added by the DSession as they are brought up and
> removed either because of a dead node or normal shutdown.  This
> number is primarily used to know when the initial collection is
> completed.

```
node2collection

Map of nodes and their test collection.  All
collections should always be identical.

node2pending

Map of nodes and the indices of their pending
tests.  The indices are an index into .pending (which is
identical to their own collection stored in
.node2collection).

collection

The one collection once it is validated to be
identical between all the nodes.  It is initialised to None
until .schedule() is called.

pending

List of indices of globally pending tests.  These are
tests which have not yet been allocated to a chunk for a node
to process.

log

A py.log.Producer instance.

config

Config object, used for handling hooks.
```

functions.name: xdist.dsession.get_default_max_worker_restart
documentation:
Gets the default value of --max-worker-restart option if it is not provided.

Use a reasonable default to avoid workers from restarting endlessly due to crashing collections (#226).

functions.name: xdist.dsession.get_workers_status_line
documentation:
Return the line to display during worker setup/collection based on the
status of the workers and number of tests collected for each.

classes.name: xdist.dsession.Interrupted
documentation: signals an immediate interruption.

classes.name: xdist.dsession.DSession

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.session_finished
documentation:
Return True if the distributed session has finished.

This means all nodes have executed all test items.  This is
used by pytest_runtestloop to break out of its loop.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.pytest_sessionstart
documentation:
Creates and starts the nodes.

The nodes are setup to put their events onto self.queue.  As
soon as nodes start they will emit the worker_workerready event.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.pytest_sessionfinish
documentation: Shutdown all nodes.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.loop_once
documentation: Process one callback from one of the workers.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_workerready
documentation:
Emitted when a node first starts up.

This adds the node to the scheduler, nodes continue with
collection without any further input.
callbacks for processing events from workers

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_workerfinished
documentation:
Emitted when node executes its pytest_sessionfinish hook.

Removes the node from the scheduler.

The node might not be in the scheduler if it had not emitted
workerready before shutdown was triggered.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_internal_error
documentation:
pytest_internalerror() was called on the worker.

pytest_internalerror() arguments are an excinfo and an excrepr, which can't
be serialized, so we go with a poor man's solution of raising an exception
here ourselves using the formatted message.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_errordown
documentation: Emitted by the WorkerController when a node dies.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_collectionfinish
documentation:
Worker has finished test collection.

This adds the collection for this node to the scheduler.  If
the scheduler indicates collection is finished (i.e. all
initial nodes have submitted their collections), then tells the
scheduler to schedule the collected items.  When initiating
scheduling the first time it logs which scheduler is in use.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_logstart
documentation: Emitted when a node calls the pytest_runtest_logstart hook.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_logfinish
documentation: Emitted when a node calls the pytest_runtest_logfinish hook.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_testreport
documentation: Emitted when a node calls the pytest_runtest_logreport hook.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_runtest_protocol_complete
documentation:
Emitted when a node fires the 'runtest_protocol_complete' event,
signalling that a test has completed the runtestprotocol and should be
removed from the pending list in the scheduler.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_unscheduled
documentation:
Emitted when a node fires the 'unscheduled' event, signalling that
some tests have been removed from the worker's queue and should be
sent to some worker again.

This should happen only in response to 'steal' command, so schedulers
not using 'steal' command don't have to implement it.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_collectreport
documentation:
Emitted when a node calls the pytest_collectreport hook.

Because we only need the report when there's a failure/skip, as optimization
we only expect to receive failed/skipped reports from workers (#330).

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession.worker_warning_recorded
documentation: Emitted when a node calls the pytest_warning_recorded hook.

methods.name: xdist.dsession.DSession._clone_node
documentation:
Return new node based on an existing one.

This is normally for when a node dies, this will copy the spec
of the existing node and create a new one with a new id.  The
new node will have been setup so it will start calling the
"worker_*" hooks and do work soon.

A pytest plugin which runs a distributed test session.

At the beginning of the test session this creates a NodeManager
instance which creates and starts all nodes.  Nodes then emit
events processed in the pytest_runtestloop hook using the worker_*
methods.

Once a node is started it will automatically start running the
pytest mainloop with some custom hooks.  This means a node
automatically starts collecting tests.  Once tests are collected
it will wait for instructions.

classes.name: xdist.dsession.WorkerStatus
documentation: Status of each worker during creation/collection.
